key_insight: InvDesMobility adds a reliability gate to closed-loop inverse materials design so only independently validated first-principles results are fed back into learning.
engineering_relevance: The framework improves trust, traceability, and decision quality for expensive materials discovery workflows, especially when the target property is a composite quantity like carrier mobility.
method: Multi-agent automated DFT generates and validates candidate 2D materials.
Evidence stratification separates relaxed structures for the generative model from retained mobility channels for the acquisition model.
Auditable release admits feedback only after QC and channel-level reliability checks.
evidence_signal: On 516 2DMatPedia-derived candidates, the workflow retained 280 QC-passed materials and 573 mobility seed channels, then screened 2.4 × 10^6 structures and submitted 102 candidates for DFT validation.
watch_item: The added gating and provenance steps may increase workflow complexity and could slow iteration if validation criteria are too strict or inconsistently calibrated.
paper_title: InvDesMobility: a reliability-gated first-principles feedback framework for closed-loop materials discovery
